reasoning: The investigation began by analyzing the root cause ranked list, which strongly pointed to the A1 interface as the primary source of anomaly, with high-precision indicators including both latency and retransmissions. While anomalies were also detected on the E2 and F1-u interfaces, these are downstream from the A1 interface in the O-RAN control hierarchy. Applying the Causal Precedence heuristic, the failure originates at the highest level of the management plane (Non-RT RIC $ightarrow$ Near-RT RIC). Severe degradation (retransmissions and latency) on the A1 interface logically precedes the subsequent performance degradation observed in the RIC-to-DU/CU (E2) and F1-u (F1) paths. The volumetric drops in throughput on lower interfaces are treated as symptomatic noise resulting from the lack of proper policy updates and control signaling from the Non-RT RIC.
actionable_steps: Inspect the network link between the Non-RT RIC and the Near-RT RIC for packet loss, latency spikes, or service crashes in the A1 agent.
